question: In Video Editor, how do you modify keyframe properties for a timeline segment and what confirms the changes?
answer: Right-click on a timeline segment to open a context menu with keyframe management options, select a modification option to adjust properties, and observe the screen update to reflect the new keyframe configuration for accurate video quality representation.
keypoints: The user right-clicks on a segment of the timeline to access additional options related to keyframe management.
A context menu appears, offering various actions for refining and adjusting keyframe settings within the video clip.
The user selects an option from this menu to modify keyframe properties, enhancing the precision of video editing tasks.
The screen updates to reflect any changes made to the keyframe configuration, ensuring accurate representation of video quality throughout the editing process.

question: In Video Editor, what happens when dragging the timeline cursor between keyframes and regular frames?
answer: Dragging the timeline cursor across the video duration shows smooth transitions and visual detail changes between keyframes and regular frames, with the screen dynamically reflecting content variations during navigation.
keypoints: The user drags the timeline cursor across the video duration, observing the transition between keyframes and regular frames.
The screen reflects smooth transitions and changes in visual details corresponding to the movement along the timeline.
